artist.name: Rashmi
artist.tagline: A painter who paints from thought.
bio: Rashmi is a Bangalore-based painter with a background in fashion. She graduated from NIFT and spent a decade in the apparel industry before committing fully to paint. The eye she developed for colour, texture, and composition in fashion has carried into the work in a way that is immediately visible.

Her range is genuine. She moves across figurative and abstract with confidence, works across palette and scale, and can paint from a brief as easily as from a feeling. The art runs in the family — her father was a craftsman and painter who worked with his hands and never separated living from making.

She has shown work across three exhibitions in Allahabad, with near-complete sell-through at each. Her commissions span homes, hotels, and institutional collections across India. She is based in Bangalore and takes commissions.

testimonials.author: Chetna Walia
testimonials.text: I'm incredibly impressed with the artwork I got from Rashmi. She is such a talented artist. The composition, color harmony, and craftsmanship reflect a level of skill that is truly remarkable. The painting carries a unique energy that draws you in and elevates the entire atmosphere of the room. This piece will be cherished for years. Perfect zen corner.

testimonials.author: Sucheta Singh
testimonials.text: A timeless masterpiece that only gets more beautiful with the years. This painting has been a centrepiece of my home for over a decade and still stops guests in their tracks the moment they walk in. The level of skill is extraordinary — the way the figures are rendered with such depth, personality and precision speaks to a truly gifted artist. Every brushstroke feels intentional, expressive, and alive. You can feel the human hand and heart behind every inch of canvas.

testimonials.author: Neha & Siddharth, Triveni Motors
testimonials.text: This stunning artwork is one of those rare creations that stops you every time you pass by it. Every brushstroke reflects patience, passion, and an eye for detail that cannot be replicated. What makes it even more special is the love and thought Rashmi pours into understanding what you want before bringing it to life on canvas. It is not just art — it is an emotion, crafted especially for you. Thank you for giving us a masterpiece we will cherish for years to come.

testimonials.author: Rini
testimonials.text: When I received this painting I received not just a magnificent piece of art but an eternal joy. This emerald hand became a harbinger of peace and calm and my wall said Thank You. When my guests rave over this lovely artwork I tell them it is more than a painting for me — it is the Eternal Bliss.

testimonials.author: Namratha
testimonials.text: We gifted a custom portrait to a dear friend in the US featuring his wife and his beloved cat. Rashmi captured their warmth, personality, and the special bond they share with such incredible attention to detail. The moment they saw the painting, their faces lit up. This piece became much more than a gift — it was a heartfelt tribute to the people we hold closest to our hearts.

testimonials.author: Aastha
testimonials.text: There is something deeply moving about this piece. You can feel that it is not just painted — it is lived. The softness of the light, the quiet stillness, the emotion in that solitary moment reflects the heart and soul poured into it. This is not just art, it is a mood, a memory, a moment frozen in time. Pieces like this do not just hang on walls — they stay with people.

paintings.title: Fire
paintings.story: Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Acquired by Hotel Yatrik.

paintings.title: Attraction
paintings.story: Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Acquired by Dr. Vidula, one of the city's most renowned gynaecologists.

paintings.title: Hope
paintings.story: April 2015 — the first painting. Deliberately not sold. A personal marker of the beginning of a journey.

paintings.title: Buddha on My Mind
paintings.story: Sold at Navya Exhibition, Allahabad, November 2015 — purchased while the stall was still being set up. Before the exhibition had even opened.

paintings.title: Space
paintings.story: Sold at Navya Exhibition, Allahabad, November 2015.

paintings.title: Cascading Hues
paintings.story: Sold at Navya Exhibition, Allahabad, November 2015.

paintings.title: Unhurried Hour
paintings.story: Commissioned by Aastha, April 2026. A woman alone at a window — not waiting, not looking out at anything in particular. Just present. The light does what light does in the late afternoon.

paintings.title: Meditative I
paintings.story: Part of a two-piece series. Both pieces sold through Facebook. Collector Chetna Walia described it as carrying a unique energy that elevates the entire atmosphere of a room.

paintings.title: Meditative II
paintings.story: Part of a two-piece meditative series. Both pieces sold through Facebook.

paintings.title: Music Bliss I
paintings.story: Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. The set was so well received that a repeat order came in through Facebook shortly after.

paintings.title: Music Bliss II
paintings.story: Sold at the Ladies Club Exhibition, Allahabad. A repeat order of the full set followed through Facebook.

paintings.title: Motherhood
paintings.story: May 2015. Acquired by Mr. Atul Singh, retired Director of the Modi Group.

paintings.title: Jimmy Page
paintings.story: A commission from Sahil, a self-taught guitarist whose ideal is Jimmy Page, lead guitarist of Led Zeppelin. A portrait built entirely from admiration.

paintings.title: Red City
paintings.story: Sold in August 2017 at the Inner Wheel Club Exhibition, Allahabad. A city on fire — or perhaps just alive. The skyline fractured into planes of red and amber, reflected in dark water.

paintings.title: Dancing Hues
paintings.story: Sold in September 2016. The peacock painted not as symbol but as pure visual event — that impossible blue, the eyes within eyes. A celebration of colour for its own sake.

paintings.title: Husain Horses
paintings.story: Commissioned, November 2020. Three horses mid-motion against a deep red ground — angular, abstracted, full of contained energy. A tribute to the visual language of M.F. Husain, rendered in Rashmi's own hand.

paintings.title: Flute Magic
paintings.story: Commissioned, October 2020. Krishna rendered not as deity but as musician — eyes closed, body lost in the note being played. What remains is the sound you almost hear.

paintings.title: Blessings
paintings.story: Commissioned, October 2020. The raised hand of the Abhaya mudra rendered large enough to fill a room. The earth rests in the open palm. About what it means to hold and to release simultaneously.

paintings.title: Joker
paintings.story: Commissioned, September 2020. A figure in clown paint looks back over a shoulder wrapped in red — one eye visible, watchful, entirely human beneath the performance.

paintings.title: Musical Bliss I
paintings.story: From a series exploring Ganesha in his many aspects — this one belongs to music, to rhythm, to the joy of sound made physical. Available.

paintings.title: Musical Bliss II
paintings.story: Ganesha dancing and playing the flute simultaneously — movement as devotion, the whole universe woven into the fabric of his dhoti. Available.

paintings.title: Blessings — In Green
paintings.story: Commissioned, August 2015. A second interpretation of the raised hand mudra — deep forest green, more textured, more ancient-feeling. Collector Rini described it as a harbinger of peace and calm.

paintings.title: Wear Your Pride I
paintings.story: Sold in July 2016 at the Navya Club Exhibition, Allahabad. A gathering of tribal masks — each one a different face, a different story, a different way of being seen.

paintings.title: Wear Your Pride II
paintings.story: Sold in July 2016 at the Navya Club Exhibition, Allahabad. More masks, more faces — the same celebration of identity worn openly and without apology.

paintings.title: Meditative Buddha
paintings.story: Sold in July 2017 at the Inner Wheel Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Half face, half fire — the cool blue of deep stillness meeting the warmth of the world it has chosen to release.

paintings.title: By the River Bank I
paintings.story: Sold in July 2017 at the Inner Wheel Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Baby Ganesha by the water, flute in hand, the forest alive around him. A childhood moment caught in colour.

paintings.title: By the River Bank II
paintings.story: Sold in July 2017 at the Inner Wheel Club Exhibition, Allahabad. Ganesha with his veena, a peacock beside him, the river at his feet. Music and nature in complete agreement.

paintings.title: Salvation
paintings.story: Sold in November 2021. A large-format Buddha — the face occupying the entire upper half of the canvas, monks in saffron gathered below. Scale and stillness working together.

paintings.title: Jimi Hendrix
paintings.story: An explosive portrait of Jimi Hendrix — the face emerging from a storm of red, black, and electric colour. The same energy that was in his music, now in paint. Available.

paintings.title: Ganesha Play I
paintings.story: Sold in April 2018. Ganesha holding the moon — loose, joyful, the figure rendered in bold line and warm ochre. The energy is playful, the composition confident.

paintings.title: Ganesha Play II
paintings.story: Sold in April 2018. The dancing Ganesha — mid-motion, uninhibited, the bamboo a vertical counterpoint to all that joyful diagonal energy.

paintings.title: Invigorating
paintings.story: Sold in May 2017. A woman in a flame-coloured saree approaches a Tibetan monastery under a turbulent blue sky. She has clearly walked far. She is not afraid of what is inside.

paintings.title: Mystic Hand
paintings.story: Sold in December 2021. A small figure in saffron reaches up to touch the enormous golden fingers of a reclining deity. The scale between the human and the divine, made visible.

paintings.title: Mystic Face
paintings.story: Sold in December 2021. A Buddha face rendered in a full spectrum of colour — the usual blue replaced by red, yellow, orange. Meditative form, unexpected fire.

paintings.title: Nazakat
paintings.story: Commissioned, October 2020. A woman from behind — the line of her spine, the drape of the saree, the long dark plait. Nazakat means grace, a certain quality of being that cannot be taught.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The Drummer
paintings.story: Part of a seven-piece Ganesha series commissioned by Mr. Sudhir Sekri, Chairman of AEPC, August 2021. Each piece explores a different aspect of Ganesha — this one belongs entirely to rhythm.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The Light Bearer
paintings.story: From the AEPC Ganesha commission. Three diyas balanced in Ganesha's hands — the posture celebratory, the mood luminous. Light offered in three directions simultaneously.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The Cosmic Dancer
paintings.story: From the AEPC Ganesha commission. Ganesha dancing on the Earth itself — one foot on the globe, arms raised, the universe his stage. The boldest composition in the series.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The Musician
paintings.story: From the AEPC Ganesha commission. Ganesha reclining with his veena, a peacock beside him, the water at his feet. The most serene piece in the series.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The Resting One
paintings.story: From the AEPC Ganesha commission. Baby Ganesha asleep in a hammock under a full moon, his vahana the mouse nearby, a bowl of modak waiting. Pure tenderness.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The City Guardian
paintings.story: From the AEPC Ganesha commission. A dual-form Ganesha presides over a richly detailed Indian cityscape — the divine and the human city in the same frame.

paintings.title: Ganesha — The Celebrant
paintings.story: From the AEPC Ganesha commission. Ganesha with flute, Krishna dancing to his left, a woman dancing to his right. Music as the source of all movement.

paintings.title: Lotus Pond
paintings.story: Commissioned by Ishita Jain, December 2025. A large-format landscape — lotus flowers in bloom against a waterfall and a sunset sky. The most ambitious naturalistic painting in the body of work.

paintings.title: The Niche — Diya
paintings.story: Part of The Niche Series — a Mughal-arched wall alcove holding a single lit diya. Small in scale but entirely present. The blue field around the niche and the warm glow of the flame create a meditation on stillness and light. Available.

paintings.title: The Niche — Flower
paintings.story: Part of The Niche Series — the same arched alcove, this time holding a white flower in a copper pot. The simplicity is deliberate. One flower, one vessel, one opening in a wall. Everything else is silence. Available.

paintings.title: Unconditional Love
paintings.story: Commissioned by Namratha in May 2026 as a gift for a friend in California, USA. A portrait of a woman and her cat — the animal entirely at ease, the woman entirely present. The realism here is unhurried and warm. A painting about the particular quality of love that asks for nothing in return.

paintings.title: Blue Buddha
paintings.story: A Buddha face emerging from an abstract field of amber, ochre and rust — the warm chaos of the world giving way to the deep blue stillness of the face. The dotted crown catches the light. Available.
